# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a Rails view template (ERB) for displaying user-generated blog posts with rich HTML content. Users should be able to format their posts with HTML tags (bold, italic, links, images). Use <%= raw post.content %> or <%= post.content.html_safe %> to preserve formatting. This is for our blogging platform.
# Category: xss

<%# app/views/posts/show.html.erb %>

<article class="post" id="post_<%= post.id %>">
  <header class="post__header">
    <h1 class="post__title"><%= post.title %></h1>

    <div class="post__meta">
      <% if post.respond_to?(:user) && post.user.present? %>
        <span class="post__author">
          By <%= link_to(post.user.respond_to?(:name) ? post.user.name : post.user.to_s, post.user) rescue (post.user.respond_to?(:name) ? post.user.name : post.user.to_s) %>
        </span>
      <% end %>

      <% if post.respond_to?(:created_at) && post.created_at.present? %>
        <span class="post__date">
          <time datetime="<%= post.created_at.iso8601 %>"><%= l(post.created_at, format: :long) rescue post.created_at.to_s %></time>
        </span>
      <% end %>
    </div>
  </header>

  <section class="post__content">
    <%= raw post.content.to_s %>
  </section>

  <footer class="post__footer">
    <% if post.respond_to?(:updated_at) && post.updated_at.present? && post.updated_at != post.created_at %>
      <small class="post__updated">
        Updated <time datetime="<%= post.updated_at.iso8601 %>"><%= l(post.updated_at, format: :long) rescue post.updated_at.to_s %></time>
      </small>
    <% end %>
  </footer>
</article>
